---
title: "Artifacts"
---

An *artifact* is a single deliverable produced for a module (or for the whole
course): a slide deck, a handout, an assignment, notes, a syllabus, etc.

Artifacts are declared in `course.yaml` under a module's `artifacts:` list or
at the top-level `artifacts:` list for course-wide documents.

---

## Artifact fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | `{type}-{index}` | Unique identifier within the scope (module or course) |
| `type` | ŌĆö | Drives template resolution and subdirectory placement |
| `enabled` | `true` | Whether to generate a stub and include in Quarto configs |
| `status` | `planned` | Lifecycle status ŌĆö metadata only |
| `file` | computed | Explicit output path; overrides the computed default |
| `output_formats` | ŌĆö | List of render formats: `revealjs`, `beamer`, `pdf`, `website` |
| `stub_template` | auto-resolved | Jinja2 template name: string or per-format dict |

---

## Default file paths

When `file:` is not set, the generator computes:

```
content/{subdir}/{scope_id}-{artifact_type}.qmd
```

where `{subdir}` is determined by artifact type:

| Artifact type | Subdirectory |
|---------------|-------------|
| `slides` | `content/slides/` |
| `handout` | `content/handouts/` |
| `notes` | `content/notes/` |
| `assignment` | `content/assignments/` |
| `syllabus` | `content/` |
| any other | `content/{type}/` |

**Example:** module `id: intro` with artifact `type: slides` produces
`content/slides/intro-slides.qmd`.

---

## Output formats

Each artifact declares which Quarto render formats it participates in via the
`output_formats` list.
The generator uses this to include the artifact in the correct sub-project
`_quarto.yml` and to emit matching format blocks in the stub front matter.

| Format | Sub-project | Output |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| `revealjs` | `content/slides/` | reveal.js HTML slides |
| `beamer` | `content/slides/` | Beamer PDF slides |
| `pdf` | `content/handouts/` or `content/assignments/` | PDF document |
| `website` | root website project | HTML page embedded in course website |

---

## The "Other Formats" sidebar link

When a Quarto document declares **multiple formats** in its YAML front matter,
Quarto automatically renders an "Other Formats" entry in the right-hand sidebar,
linking to the alternative output (e.g. a PDF download link on an HTML page).

Whether this link appears is controlled entirely by the **stub template**, not
by the renderer.
The built-in templates conditionally emit a second format block based on
`output_formats`:

| Template | Condition | Emitted formats in stub |
|----------|-----------|------------------------|
| `handout.qmd.j2` | `website` in `output_formats` | `html: default` + `pdf: ŌĆ”` |
| `handout.qmd.j2` | `website` **not** in `output_formats` | `pdf: ŌĆ”` only |
| `notes.qmd.j2` | `pdf` in `output_formats` | `html: default` + `pdf: ŌĆ”` |
| `notes.qmd.j2` | `pdf` **not** in `output_formats` | `html: default` only |
| `assignment.qmd.j2` | always | `html: default` + `pdf: ŌĆ”` |

To enable the "Other Formats: PDF" link for a handout, declare both formats:

```yaml
artifacts:
  - id: intro-handout
    type: handout
    output_formats: [website, pdf]   # both ŌåÆ "Other Formats: PDF" appears
```

Custom stub templates follow the same rule: declare both formats in the
generated front matter and Quarto produces the sidebar link automatically.

---

## Examples

### Slides with Beamer PDF

```yaml
artifacts:
  - id: intro-slides
    type: slides
    enabled: true
    output_formats: [revealjs, beamer]
```

### Handout with website page and PDF download

```yaml
artifacts:
  - id: intro-handout
    type: handout
    enabled: true
    output_formats: [website, pdf]
```

### Assignment (website + PDF)

```yaml
artifacts:
  - id: ex01
    type: assignment
    enabled: true
    output_formats: [website, pdf]
    lms:
      release: 2026-10-05
      due: 2026-10-19
```

### Disabled artifact (placeholder for future use)

```yaml
artifacts:
  - id: project-brief
    type: assignment
    enabled: false
```

### Pinning a custom template

```yaml
artifacts:
  - id: my-poster
    type: slides
    output_formats: [revealjs]
    stub_template: poster.qmd.j2              # single template for all formats

  - id: lecture-slides
    type: slides
    output_formats: [revealjs, beamer]
    stub_template:
      default: slides.qmd.j2
      beamer:  slides-beamer.qmd.j2          # per-format override
```

See [Templates & Filters](templates.qmd) for full template resolution rules.
